Projects.title: NSS Space Settlement Contest & ISDC
desc: Participated in the globally recognized NSS Space Settlement Contest, applying advanced principles of physics and aerospace engineering to conceptualize a viable, self-sustaining human habitat in orbit.

Attended the International Space Development Conference (ISDC), networking with industry professionals and gaining deep insights into orbital mechanics, space policy, sustainable resource management, and the future of human exploration beyond Earth.

Projects.title: B.Tech Aerospace Engineering Academic Pathway
desc: Dedicated to pursuing a Bachelor of Technology in Aerospace Engineering. Currently mastering foundational engineering disciplines including Advanced Mathematics (Calculus/Algebra), Classical Mechanics, and Thermodynamics through competitive JEE (Joint Entrance Examination) preparation.

Following a rigorous standard curriculum, achieving high percentiles in JEE Mains Phase 1, actively targeting placement within premier engineering institutions known for their aerospace programs and research output.

Projects.title: Project J.A.R.V.I.S: Personal AI Assistant
desc: Architecting and developing a highly intelligent, customized Personal AI Assistant optimized for managing complex academic schedules, organizing technical documentation, and automating repetitive computational tasks.

Investigating the critical intersection of Generative AI, Machine Learning algorithms, and Aerospace Engineering—specifically exploring applications in real-time trajectory optimization, aerodynamic simulation data processing, and predictive maintenance analysis for aircraft systems.

Projects.title: Aerospace Discoveries & Physics Insights
desc: Engineering Insight 1: The legendary SR-71 Blackbird achieved cruise speeds exceeding Mach 3.2. Due to intense air friction, its external titanium skin reached temperatures above 500°C, causing the airframe to thermally expand several inches during supersonic flight.

Space Fact: While sound waves require a medium like air or water to travel, making deep space functionally silent, NASA utilizes data sonification to convert the electromagnetic waves captured from stars, nebulae, and black holes into auditory data that scientists and the public can experience.

Thermodynamic Reality: Commercial airliners cruise at roughly 35,000 feet, where the outside ambient temperature is approximately -54°C. Maintaining a safe, comfortable cabin requires advanced environmental control systems (ECS) that efficiently utilize engine 'bleed air' while managing extreme temperature differentials.

Projects.title: Creative Dimensions: Fine Arts & Music Theory
desc: Engineering excellence requires sophisticated, innovative creativity. I balance my intensive technical and mathematical studies with a deep dedication to the formal fine arts, bringing a unique, design-centric methodology to mechanical and structural problem-solving.

Credentialed with a Grade 1 Trinity College London Certificate in Electronic Keyboard, actively studying the intricate harmonic compositions and arrangements of legends such as Ilaiyaraaja. Also an experienced participant in competitive drawing, specializing in visualizing complex systems and high-end, futuristic aesthetic design concepts.
